001|# Product Requirement Document (PRD): SetYourOffer.com Backend
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python (Django)
006|- **Database:** PostgreSQL
007|- **Project Name:** set_your_offer_backend
008|
009|### Restated Requirements
010|The backend for SetYourOffer.com must provide:
011|1. A smart authentication and registration system
012|2. An ad submission workflow
013|3. An advanced search engine
014|4. AI features for data analysis and recommendations
015|
016|All user flows, data models, and feature requirements must be covered in detail.
017|
018|---
019|## 2. Product Definition
020|
021|### 2.1 Product Goals
022|- **Goal 1:** Ensure secure, seamless, and intelligent user authentication and registration.
023|- **Goal 2:** Enable efficient, user-friendly ad submission and management workflows.
024|- **Goal 3:** Provide advanced search capabilities for users to discover relevant ads quickly.
025|- **Goal 4:** Deliver AI-powered data analysis and personalized recommendations to enhance user engagement and platform value.
026|
027|### 2.2 User Stories
028|1. As a new user, I want to register quickly and securely so that I can start posting ads immediately.
029|2. As a returning user, I want to log in with smart authentication so that my account remains protected.
030|3. As an advertiser, I want to submit and manage my ads easily so that I can reach my target audience.
031|4. As a buyer, I want to search for ads using advanced filters so that I can find relevant offers efficiently.
032|5. As a user, I want to receive AI-driven recommendations so that I discover offers tailored to my interests.
033|
034|### 2.3 Competitive Analysis
035|| Product                | Pros                                         | Cons                                      |
036||------------------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
037|| Craigslist             | Large user base, simple UI                   | Limited search, no AI recommendations     |
038|| Facebook Marketplace   | Social integration, advanced search          | Privacy concerns, ad quality varies       |

001|# Product Requirement Document (PRD): SetYourOffer.com Backend
002|
003|## 1. Language & Project Info
004|- **Language:** English
005|- **Programming Language:** Python (Django)
006|- **Database:** PostgreSQL
007|- **Project Name:** set_your_offer_backend
008|
009|### Restated Requirements
010|The backend for SetYourOffer.com must provide:
011|1. A smart authentication and registration system
012|2. An ad submission workflow
013|3. An advanced search engine
014|4. AI features for data analysis and recommendations
015|
016|All user flows, data models, and feature requirements must be covered in detail.
017|
018|---
019|## 2. Product Definition
020|
021|### 2.1 Product Goals
022|- **Goal 1:** Ensure secure, seamless, and intelligent user authentication and registration.
023|- **Goal 2:** Enable efficient, user-friendly ad submission and management workflows.
024|- **Goal 3:** Provide advanced search capabilities for users to discover relevant ads quickly.
025|- **Goal 4:** Deliver AI-powered data analysis and personalized recommendations to enhance user engagement and platform value.
026|
027|### 2.2 User Stories
028|1. As a new user, I want to register quickly and securely so that I can start posting ads immediately.
029|2. As a returning user, I want to log in with smart authentication so that my account remains protected.
030|3. As an advertiser, I want to submit and manage my ads easily so that I can reach my target audience.
031|4. As a buyer, I want to search for ads using advanced filters so that I can find relevant offers efficiently.
032|5. As a user, I want to receive AI-driven recommendations so that I discover offers tailored to my interests.
033|
034|### 2.3 Competitive Analysis
035|| Product                | Pros                                         | Cons                                      |
036||------------------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|
037|| Craigslist             | Large user base, simple UI                   | Limited search, no AI recommendations     |
038|| Facebook Marketplace   | Social integration, advanced search          | Privacy concerns, ad quality varies       |
039|| eBay Classifieds       | Trusted brand, secure transactions           | Complex workflows, limited recommendations|
040|| OfferUp                | Mobile-first, user ratings                   | Search limitations, less data analytics   |
041|| Gumtree                | Local focus, easy ad posting                 | Basic search, no AI features              |
042|| OLX                    | Global reach, free listings                  | Limited personalization, basic analytics  |
043|| Letgo                  | Visual ad browsing, chat integration         | Limited AI, search not highly advanced    |
044|
045|### 2.4 Competitive Quadrant Chart
046|```mermaid
047|quadrantChart
048|    title "Marketplace Backend Feature Comparison"
049|    x-axis "Basic Features" --> "Advanced Features"
050|    y-axis "Low Intelligence" --> "High Intelligence"
051|    quadrant-1 "Expand AI & Search"
052|    quadrant-2 "Improve Intelligence"
053|    quadrant-3 "Upgrade Features"
054|    quadrant-4 "Optimize Experience"
055|    "Craigslist": [0.2, 0.2]
056|    "Facebook Marketplace": [0.7, 0.5]
057|    "eBay Classifieds": [0.6, 0.4]
058|    "OfferUp": [0.5, 0.3]
059|    "Gumtree": [0.3, 0.2]
060|    "OLX": [0.4, 0.3]
061|    "Letgo": [0.5, 0.4]
062|    "SetYourOffer.com Backend": [0.8, 0.8]
063|```
064|
065|---
066|## 3. Technical Specifications
067|
068|### 3.1 Requirements Analysis
069|The backend must support secure user authentication and registration, robust ad submission and management, advanced search capabilities, and AI-driven data analysis and recommendations. All features must be scalable, maintainable, and optimized for performance.
070|
071|#### Key Technical Needs:
072|- User authentication (email, social login, multi-factor)
073|- User registration (validation, onboarding)
074|- Ad submission (CRUD operations, media uploads, moderation)
075|- Search engine (full-text, filters, relevance ranking)
076|- AI features (recommendation engine, analytics dashboard)
077|- Data models for users, ads, sessions, recommendations
078|- API endpoints for all major workflows
079|- Admin controls and moderation tools
080|
081|### 3.2 Requirements Pool
082|- **P0 (Must-have):**
083|  - Secure authentication and registration
084|  - Ad submission and management
085|  - Advanced search engine with filters
086|  - AI-powered recommendations
087|  - Scalable API endpoints
088|- **P1 (Should-have):**
089|  - Social login integration
090|  - Ad moderation workflow
091|  - Analytics dashboard for users and admins
092|- **P2 (Nice-to-have):**
093|  - Multi-language support
094|  - Real-time notifications
095|  - Advanced reporting tools
096|
097|### 3.3 Data Models
098|- **User:** id, name, email, password_hash, social_auth, registration_date, last_login, preferences
099|- **Ad:** id, user_id, title, description, category, price, location, images, status, created_at, updated_at
100|- **Session:** id, user_id, token, created_at, expires_at
101|- **Recommendation:** id, user_id, ad_id, score, generated_at
102|- **SearchLog:** id, user_id, query, filters, timestamp
103|
104|### 3.4 User Flows
105|- **Registration:** User submits registration info → System validates → Account created → Welcome email sent
106|- **Authentication:** User enters credentials → System verifies → Access granted → Session started
107|- **Ad Submission:** User creates ad → System validates → Ad saved → Moderation (if needed) → Ad published
108|- **Search:** User enters query/filters → System processes → Relevant ads returned → User interacts with results
109|- **Recommendations:** User activity tracked → AI engine analyzes data → Personalized ads recommended
110|
111|### 3.5 UI Design Draft (Backend API)
112|- **/api/register**: POST (user info)
113|- **/api/login**: POST (credentials)
114|- **/api/ads**: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE (ad management)
115|- **/api/search**: GET (query, filters)
116|- **/api/recommendations**: GET (user_id)
117|- **/api/admin/moderate**: POST/GET (ad moderation)
118|- **/api/analytics**: GET (dashboard data)
119|
120|### 3.6 Open Questions
121|- What third-party authentication providers are required?
122|- What moderation rules should be enforced for ads?
123|- What AI models/algorithms are preferred for recommendations?
124|- Are there specific analytics metrics required by stakeholders?
125|- Is GDPR or other compliance needed for user data?
126|
127|---

As a returning user, I want to log in with smart authentication so that my account remains protected.\\n030|3. As an advertiser, I want to submit and manage my ads easily so that I can reach my target audience.\\n031|4. As a buyer, I want to search for ads using advanced filters so that I can find relevant offers efficiently.\\n032|5. As a user, I want to receive AI-driven recommendations so that I discover offers tailored to my interests.\\n033|\\n034|### 2.3 Competitive Analysis\\n035|| Product                | Pros                                         | Cons                                      |\\n036||------------------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------|\\n037|| Craigslist             | Large user base, simple UI    | Trusted brand, secure transactions           | Complex workflows, limited recommendations|\\n040|| OfferUp                | Mobile-first, user ratings                   | Search limitations, less data analytics   |\\n041|| Gumtree                | Local focus, easy ad posting                 | Basic search, no AI features              |\\n042|| OLX                    | Global reach, free listings                  | Limited personalization, basic analytics  |\\n043|| Letgo                  | Visual ad browsing, chat integration         | Limited AI, search not highly advanced User enters credentials → System verifies → Access granted → Session started\\n107|- **Ad Submission:** User creates ad → System validates → Ad saved → Moderation (if needed) → Ad published\\n108|- **Search:** User enters query/filters → System processes → Relevant ads returned → User interacts with results\\n109|- **Recommendations:** User activity tracked → AI engine analyzes data → from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import AbstractBaseUser, PermissionsMixin, BaseUserManager
from django.utils import timezone

class UserManager(BaseUserManager):
    def create_user(self, email, name, password=None, **extra_fields):
        if not email:
            raise ValueError('The Email field must be set')
        email = self.normalize_email(email)
        user = self.model(email=email, name=name, **extra_fields)
        user.set_password(password)
        user.registration_date = timezone.now()
        user.save(using=self._db)
        return user

    def create_superuser(self, email, name, password=None, **extra_fields):
        extra_fields.setdefault('is_staff', True)
        extra_fields.setdefault('is_superuser', True)
        if extra_fields.get('is_staff') is not True:
            raise ValueError('Superuser must have is_staff=True.')
        if extra_fields.get('is_superuser') is not True:
            raise ValueError('Superuser must have is_superuser=True.')
        return self.create_user(email, name, password, **extra_fields)

class User(AbstractBaseUser, PermissionsMixin):
    email = models.EmailField(unique=True, max_length=255)
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    password_hash = models.CharField(max_length=128)
    social_auth = models.JSONField(default=dict, blank=True)  # e.g., {"google": "...", "facebook": "..."}
    registration_date = models.DateTimeField(default=timezone.now)
    last_login = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    preferences = models.JSONField(default=dict, blank=True)
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    is_staff = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    # Multi-factor authentication fields
    mfa_enabled = models.BooleanField(default=False)
    mfa_secret = models.CharField(max_length=255, blank=True, null=True)
    mfa_backup_codes = models.JSONField(default=list, blank=True)

    USERNAME_FIELD = 'email'
    REQUIRED_FIELDS = ['name']

    objects = UserManager()

    def __str__(self):
        return self.email

class Session(models.Model):
    user = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='sessions')
    token = models.CharField(max_length=255, unique=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    expires_at = models.DateTimeField()

    def __str__(self):
        return f"Session for {self.user.email} ({self.token})"

an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. from django.db import models
from django.conf import settings

class AdCategory(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100, unique=True)
    description = models.TextField(blank=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.name

class Ad(models.Model):
    STATUS_CHOICES = [
        ('pending', 'Pending Moderation'),
        ('active', 'Active'),
        ('inactive', 'Inactive'),
        ('rejected', 'Rejected'),
        ('sold', 'Sold'),
    ]

    user = models.ForeignKey(
        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
        related_name='ads'
    )
    title = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    description = models.TextField()
    category = models.ForeignKey(
        AdCategory,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        related_name='ads'
    )
    price = models.DecimalField(max_digits=12, decimal_places=2)
    location = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    images = models.JSONField(default=list, blank=True)  # Store image URLs or paths
    status = models.CharField(
        max_length=20,
        choices=STATUS_CHOICES,
        default='pending'
    )
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    updated_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.title} ({self.user.email})"

class AdImage(models.Model):
    ad = models.ForeignKey(
        Ad,
        on_delete=models.CASCADE,
        related_name='ad_images'
    )
    image = models.ImageField(upload_to='ad_images/')
    uploaded_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return f"Image for Ad {self.ad.id} - {self.image.name}"

Command Engineer2.write_new_code executed: The file /home/metagpt-fuzzing/Desktop/MetaGPT/workspace/set_your_offer_backend/search/views.py has been successfully created, with content:
from rest_framework.views import APIView
from rest_framework.response import Response
from rest_framework import status, permissions
from django.db.models import Q
from django.contrib.postgres.search import SearchVector, SearchQuery, SearchRank
from ads.models import Ad, AdCategory
from ads.serializers import AdSerializer
from users.models import User
from .serializers import SearchLogSerializer
from .models import SearchLog

class AdSearchView(APIView):
    """
    Advanced search engine for ads with full-text search, filters, and relevance ranking.
    """
    permission_classes = [permissions.AllowAny]

    def get(self, request, format=None):
        query = request.query_params.get('q', '')
        category = request.query_params.get('category', None)
        min_price = request.query_params.get('min_price', None)
        max_price = request.query_params.get('max_price', None)
        location = request.query_params.get('location', None)
        sort_by = request.query_params.get('sort_by', 'relevance')  # relevance, price, created_at

        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status='active')

        # Full-text search
        if query:
            search_vector = SearchVector('title', weight='A') + SearchVector('description', weight='B')
            search_query = SearchQuery(query)
            ads = ads.annotate(
                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)
            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by('-rank')

        # Category filter
        if category:
            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)

        # Price filters
        if min_price:
            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)
        if max_price:
            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)

        # Location filter
        if location:
            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)

        # Sorting
        if sort_by == 'price':
            ads = ads.order_by('price')
        elif sort_by == 'created_at':
            ads = ads.order_by('-created_at')
        elif sort_by == 'relevance' and query:
            ads = ads.order_by('-rank')
        else:
            ads = ads.order_by('-created_at')

        # Log the search
        if request.user.is_authenticated:
            log_data = {
                'user': request.user.id,
                'query': query,
                'filters': {
                    'category': category,
                    'min_price': min_price,
                    'max_price': max_price,
                    'location': location,
                    'sort_by': sort_by,
                }
            }
            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)
            if serializer.is_valid():
                serializer.save()

        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)
        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)

relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. Instead, use the Editor.goto_line command. It\'s much quicker. \n2. Always make sure to look at the currently open file and the current working directory (which appears right after the currently open file). The currently open file might be in a different directory than the working directory! Note that some commands, such as \'create\', open files, so they might change the current open file.\n3. When using Editor.edit_file_by_replace, if there is no exact match, take the difference in indentation into consideration.\n4. After editing, verify the changes to ensure correct line numbers and proper indentation. Adhere to PEP8 standards for Python code.\n5. NOTE ABOUT THE EDIT COMMAND: Indentation really matters! When editing a file, make sure to insert appropriate indentation before each line! Ensuring the code adheres to PEP8 standards. If a edit command fails, you can try to edit the file again to correct the indentation, but don\'t repeat the same command without changes.\n6. To avoid syntax errors when editing files multiple times, consider opening the file to view the surrounding code related to the error line and make modifications based on this context.\n7. Ensure to observe the currently open file and the current working directory, which is displayed right after the open file. The open file might be in a different directory than the working directory. Remember, commands like \'create\' open files and might alter the current open file.\n8. Effectively using Use search commands (`search_dir`, `search_file`, `find_file`) and navigation commands (`open_file`, `goto_line`) to locate and modify files efficiently. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # an existing plan to achieve the goal. A plan consists of one to 3 tasks.\nIf plan is created, you should track the progress and update the plan accordingly, such as Plan.finish_current_task, Plan.append_task, Plan.reset_task, Plan.replace_task, etc.\nWhen presented a current task, tackle the task using the available commands.\nPay close attention to new user message, review the conversation history, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to respond to new user requirement.\nNote:\n1. If you keeping encountering errors, unexpected situation, or you are not sure of proceeding, use RoleZero.ask_human to ask for help.\n2. Carefully review your progress at the current task, if your actions so far has not fulfilled the task instruction, you should continue with current task. Otherwise, finish current task by Plan.finish_current_task explicitly.\n3. Each time you finish a task, use RoleZero.reply_to_human to report your progress.\n4. Don\'t forget to append task first when all existing tasks are finished and new tasks are required.\n5. Avoid repeating tasks you have already completed. And end loop when all requirements are met.\nYou are an autonomous programmer\n\nThe special interface consists of a file editor that shows you 100 lines of a file at a time.\n\nYou can use terminal commands (e.g., cat, ls, cd) by calling Terminal.run_command.\n\nYou should carefully observe the behavior and results of the previous action, and avoid triggering repeated errors.\n\nIn addition to the terminal, I also provide additional tools. \n\nIf provided an issue link, you first action must be navigate to the issue page using Browser tool to understand the issue.\n\nYour must check if the repository exists at the current path. If it exists, navigate to the repository path. If the repository doesn\'t exist, please download it and then navigate to it.\nAll subsequent actions must be performed within this repository path. Do not leave this directory to execute any actions at any time.\n\nNote:\n\n1. If you open a file and need to get to an area around a specific line that is not in the first 100 lines, say line 583, don\'t just use the scroll_down command multiple times. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # an existing plan to achieve the goal. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # Log the search\n        if request.user.is_authenticated:\n            log_data = {\n                \'user\': request.user.id,\n                \'query\': query,\n                \'filters\': {\n                    \'category\': category,\n                    \'min_price\': min_price,\n                    \'max_price\': max_price,\n                    \'location\': location,\n                    \'sort_by\': sort_by,\n                }\n            }\n            serializer = SearchLogSerializer(data=log_data)\n            if serializer.is_valid():\n                serializer.save()\n\n        serializer = AdSerializer(ads, many=True)\n        return Response(serializer.data, status=status.HTTP_200_OK)\n\nCommand Plan.finish_current_task executed:Current task is finished. relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        # relevance, price, created_at\n\n        ads = Ad.objects.filter(status=\'active\')\n\n        # Full-text search\n        if query:\n            search_vector = SearchVector(\'title\', weight=\'A\') + SearchVector(\'description\', weight=\'B\')\n            search_query = SearchQuery(query)\n            ads = ads.annotate(\n                rank=SearchRank(search_vector, search_query)\n            ).filter(rank__gte=0.1).order_by(\'-rank\')\n\n        # Category filter\n        if category:\n            ads = ads.filter(category__name__iexact=category)\n\n        # Price filters\n        if min_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__gte=min_price)\n        if max_price:\n            ads = ads.filter(price__lte=max_price)\n\n        # Location filter\n        if location:\n            ads = ads.filter(location__icontains=location)\n\n        # Sorting\n        if sort_by == \'price\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'price\')\n        elif sort_by == \'created_at\':\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n        elif sort_by == \'relevance\' and query:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-rank\')\n        else:\n            ads = ads.order_by(\'-created_at\')\n\n        #
